case_title: JERVIS MEDICAL STAFFING, LLC VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA ET AL

docket_entries.description: DECLARATION OF TOM PROUNTZOS RE DEMURRER OF THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA TO COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 58439388) FILED BY DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER OF THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA TO COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 58625806) FILED BY DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA
REMOVED FROM MASTER JURY CALENDAR SET FOR JUN-26-2017 - CASE SETTLED WITH COURT SETTLEMENT COMMISSIONER, PANG LY. ASSIGNED TO DEPT. 610 ON AUG-29-2017 AT 10:30 AM FOR DISMISSAL PER CRC 3.1385. (206)
COURT REPORTING SERVICES LESS THAN 1 HOUR (TRANSACTION ID # 58439388) FILED BY DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO ALL FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION. ALL FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION ARE ADEQUATELY PLED AGAINST THE REGENTS. AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT, LIBERALLY CONSTRUING PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS, PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT THE REGENTS BREACHED SECTION 6.1 OF THE CONTRACT AND THAT SECTION 6.3 WAS NOT APPLICABLE. AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE IMPLIED COVENANT, LIBERALLY CONSTRUING PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS, PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THAT, IF SECTION 6.3 WERE APPLICABLE, THE REGENTS BREACHED THE IMPLIED COVENANT BY EXERCISING ITS DISCRETION IN A MANNER THAT WAS CONTRARY TO THE PURPOSES OF SECTION 6.3 AND DEPRIVED PLAINTIFF OF THE BENEFITS OF THE CONTRACT. THE MOVING PAPERS FAIL TO CITE ANY IMMUNITY IN THE TORT CLAIMS ACT THAT BARS THE THIRD AND FOURTH CLAIMS AND THOSE CLAIMS ARE PRESUMPTIVELY COGNIZABLE PER GOVERNMENT CODE 815.2(A). THE COURT DID NOT CONSIDER THE REGENTS' ARGUMENT BASED ON GOVERNMENT CODE 820.2 SINCE THAT STATUTE WAS NEITHER CITED NOR DISCUSSED IN THE MOVING PAPERS AND PLAINTIFF HAD NO OPPORTUNITY TO RESPOND THE TO THE ARGUMENT. LIBERALLY CONSTRUING PLAINTIFF'S ALLEGATIONS, THE THIRD AND FOURTH CLAIMS SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGE THAT THE REGENTS INTERFERED WITH PLAINTIFF'S CONTRACT RIGHTS AND ECONOMIC ADVANTAGE BY INDUCING MEDASSETS TO BREACH THE CONTRACT AND BY INDUCING CNAS TO BREACH THEIR OBLIGATIONS TO PLAINTIFF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MEDASSETS PERFORMANCE MANAGEMENT SOLUTIONS' PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED AND THE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS AGAINST MEDASSETS ARE STAYED PENDING THE COMPLETION OF THE ARBITRATION. THE ARBITRATION SHALL OCCUR IN SAN FRANCISCO OR SUCH OTHER LOCATION AS THE PARTIES AGREE. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO ESTABLISH ANY SUBSTANTIVE UNCONSCIONABILITY. MR. VELEZ IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT TRACKS THE TENTATIVE RULING PURUSANT CRC 3.1312 ADDING "WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR JERVIS TO FILE AN APPLICATION THAT IT IS UNABLE TO PROCEED IN ARBITRATION DUE TO INABILITY TO PAY". CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2016 Continued To Oct-19-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendant Medassets Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Apr-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Martin Velez of Law Office of G. Martin Velez telephone number 415-342-4125 for Plaintiff Jervis Medical Staffing, LLC.
Michael Hogue of Greenberg Traurig telephone number 415-655-1303 for Defendant Medassets Performance.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Defendant MedAssets Performance Management Solutions' petition to compel arbitration is granted and the plaintiff's claims against MedAssets are stayed pending the completion of the arbitration. The arbitration shall occur in San Francisco or such other location as the parties agree. Plaintiff fails to establish any substantive unconscionability. Mr. Velez is to prepare a form of order that tracks the tentative ruling purusant CRC 3.1312 adding "without prejudice for Jervis to file an application that it is unable to proceed in arbitration due to inability to pay".

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-02-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. G. MARTIN VELEZ, ESQ. of Law Office of G. Martin Velez, Tel No. 415-342-4125 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. TOM PROUNTZOS, ESQ. of Law Offices of Goodman Neuman Hamilton LLP, Tel No. 415-705-0412.

Court makes disclosures re: attorney Josh Goodman, and the General Counsel of the Regents, but states finding no need to disqualify itself. Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled as to all four causes of action. All four causes of action are adequately pled against the Regents. As to the first cause of action for breach of contract, liberally construing plaintiff's allegations, plaintiff sufficiently alleges that the Regents breached section 6.1 of the contract and that section 6.3 was not applicable. As to the second cause of action for breach of the implied covenant, liberally construing plaintiff's allegations, plaintiff sufficiently alleges that, if section 6.3 were applicable, the Regents breached the implied covenant by exercising its discretion in a manner that was contrary to the purposes of section 6.3 and deprived plaintiff of the benefits of the contract. The moving papers fail to cite any immunity in the Tort Claims Act that bars the third and fourth claims and those claims are presumptively cognizable per Government Code 815.2(a). The court did not consider the Regents' argument based on Government Code 820.2 since that statute was neither cited nor discussed in the moving papers and plaintiff had no opportunity to respond the to the argument. Liberally construing plaintiff's allegations, the third and fourth claims sufficiently allege that the Regents interfered with plaintiff's contract rights and economic advantage by inducing MedAssets to breach the contract and by inducing CNAs to breach their obligations to plaintiff. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)
